title: Diverged Branches — Rebase vs Merge Decision
description: Your local `main` and `origin/main` have diverged — you have two commits that origin doesn't have, and origin has three commits you don't have. You need to integrate them correctly so your local branch is up to date and your commits sit on top cleanly.
objective: Local `main` must contain the three commits that were on `origin/main` plus your two local commits rebased on top. The history must be linear (no merge commit), and your local commits must appear above the remote commits. The working tree must be clean.
hints: `git fetch origin` brings down the remote changes without modifying your local branch.
`git rebase origin/main` replays your local commits on top of the fetched remote commits, keeping history linear.
Check `git log --oneline --graph` to verify the linear shape before pushing.
